Justification: The kettle shall have a functional capacity of 1.5 liters, validated through user research to serve 4-6 standard cups, balancing sustainability goals (reduced material and energy per boil) with user needs.; A 'standard cup' is defined as 250ml. This capacity offers a quantifiable reduction in material footprint (estimated 12% less steel than 1.7L) and energy demand (estimated 10% less energy per boil).; The functional maximum fill line shall be clearly marked with a durable, high-contrast indicator, visible under various lighting conditions, positioned adjacent to the handle for easy reference.

The kettle shall boil 1.5 liters of water from 20°C ambient temperature to 98°C-100°C within 4.5 minutes, enabled by a target power output of 2000W.; The kettle shall boil 0.5 liters of water from 20°C ambient temperature to 98°C-100°C within 2 minutes.; Performance deviation will be acceptable within ±1 minute for ambient temperatures ranging from 10°C to 30°C. The final boiling temperature tolerance is ±2°C.

The primary design target is a 2000W power rating for rapid boiling, with a 1500W variant available for markets prioritizing extreme energy savings, accepting a longer boiling time (approx. 5.5 minutes for 1.5L).; The target energy efficiency at 2000W shall be a minimum of 85%, measured by the ratio of energy absorbed by water to total electrical energy consumed during a full boil cycle.; The kettle will be designed for compatibility with standard household electrical circuits in target markets: 220-240V/50Hz (EU/UK) and 120V/60Hz (North America).

The primary material for the exterior casing shall be a minimum of 75% post-consumer recycled stainless steel (e.g., 304 grade), sourced according to Global Recycled Standard (GRS) certification.; The recycled stainless steel shall have a matte finish to minimize visible scratches and maintain aesthetic appeal.; The PDS prioritizes durability and recyclability. If a bioplastic is considered for future iterations, it must meet stringent durability (scratch resistance: Mohs 4, UV stability: Delta E < 2 after 200 hours, impact resistance: no cracking on 0.75m drop) and end-of-life criteria (certified industrial composting) and will be clearly designated as a distinct product variant.

The interior and heating element shall be constructed from food-grade 304 stainless steel with a minimum thickness of 0.6mm.; A minimum of 30% recycled content will be incorporated into the stainless steel, with verification through supplier certifications. Contingency plans will be in place if this target cannot be met without compromising food-grade certification.; The interior surface shall feature a smooth, electropolished finish to enhance cleaning and reduce limescale adhesion.

The kettle shall consume less than 0.2W in standby mode after auto-shutoff, measured with all indicator lights active.; This target will be validated using standardized testing protocols for low-power electronics.

The auto shut-off mechanism shall activate when water reaches 98°C-100°C or immediately upon lifting the kettle from its base.; The lift-off detection sensor shall be rated for a minimum of 5,000 cycles and respond within 1 second. The primary shut-off thermostat shall be rated for 3,000 cycles.

The boil-dry protection shall deactivate the heating element within 5 seconds of detecting no water.; Upon activation, a single audible beep (approx. 70 dB at 0.5m) and a flashing red indicator light will signal the condition. Manual reset via unplugging and replugging is required after the unit has cooled.

The kettle shall be designed to withstand a minimum of 3,000 full boiling cycles (1.5L from 20°C to 100°C).; Key components such as the thermostat and heating element shall be rated for a minimum of 5,000 cycles.; Validation will be performed through accelerated life testing simulating 3,000 cycles. A 2-year warranty will be provided.

The kettle, when empty and at ambient temperature, shall withstand three drops from 0.5 meters onto a tiled surface in random orientations without functional failure or casing cracks that compromise safety.; For a full kettle, a controlled drop from 0.3 meters onto a tiled surface shall not result in functional failure or detachment of the base or handle.

The handle shall be constructed from heat-resistant, non-slip polymer (e.g., PP or ABS with textured finish).; Maximum handle surface temperature shall not exceed 45°C during operation. A secure grip shall be achievable with a maximum force of 20N.; The handle-to-body connection shall withstand a static load of 150N. A simulated pouring test at 45° tilt with a full kettle shall demonstrate stability and control.

The spout shall be designed for drip-free pouring, with a maximum allowable drip volume of 0.5ml within 10 seconds post-pour.; The spout opening shall facilitate a flow rate of 100-150 ml/second at a 45° pouring angle, minimizing splashing.; The interior of the spout shall be smooth and accessible for cleaning with a standard bottle brush.

The lid shall open via a single button press requiring ≤ 5N force, or manually requiring ≤ 10N force.; The lid shall open to a minimum angle of 75° for ease of filling and cleaning.; The lid seal shall prevent steam leakage exceeding 5% of total steam volume during boiling, tested under simulated conditions.

The kettle shall have an internal opening diameter of ≥ 80mm for easy access.; The interior surface shall be smooth stainless steel, designed for easy descaling with standard solutions, targeting a requirement of descaling no more than once every 2 months under typical hard water conditions.; The removable filter shall be dishwasher safe (top rack). The exterior shall be easily wiped clean with a damp cloth.

A clear, high-contrast water level indicator shall display markings for 0.5L (minimum) and 1.5L (maximum fill), accurate to within ±5%.; The indicator window shall be made of durable, scratch-resistant borosilicate glass, positioned for easy viewing from a standing user's perspective.; Markings shall be permanently etched or laser-marked for longevity.

The power base shall include a cord storage system with at least three pegs for winding the 0.75-meter power cord.; The cord shall be secured by a clip or friction fit to prevent unraveling during storage.

The kettle shall operate at a maximum noise level of 58 dB during the final stages of boiling (measured at 1 meter distance with a full kettle from 20°C).; Noise reduction will be achieved through optimized heating element design and internal baffling, validated via acoustic testing in a simulated room environment.

The kettle shall feature a modern, minimalist design with clean lines, a matte finish (e.g., brushed stainless steel, matte white, charcoal grey), and a visually unobtrusive form factor.; Tactile qualities will be considered, aiming for a smooth, premium feel without being slippery.; Specific visual references and mood boards will be provided to define the aesthetic direction.

Packaging shall consist of 100% post-consumer recycled cardboard and soy-based inks, with minimal plastic use restricted to recycled and recyclable protective elements.; Packaging weight shall not exceed 15% of the product's net weight, and volume will be minimized for efficient shipping.; All packaging materials will be clearly marked with appropriate recycling symbols.

The kettle shall be designed for disassembly using standard tools, with components joined by screws or snap-fits where feasible, minimizing adhesive use.; Major plastic and metal components will be labeled according to ISO 11469 standards to facilitate recycling.; Key spare parts (e.g., lid mechanism, heating element) will be available for a minimum of 5 years post-production to promote repairability. Partnerships with certified e-waste recyclers will be established.

The kettle shall achieve CE, RoHS, and Energy Star certifications (meeting current criteria).; Regional safety certifications such as UL (North America) or equivalent will be obtained based on target markets.; Consideration will be given to ISO 14001 certification for the manufacturing facility.

Variable temperature control shall offer settings for 70°C, 80°C, 90°C, and 100°C, with an accuracy of ±2°C.; An optional 'keep warm' function will maintain the selected temperature for up to 30 minutes, consuming no more than 10W.; The user interface will feature intuitive buttons and clear LED indicators for ease of use, justified by enhanced beverage preparation and reduced energy waste from re-boiling.

A single, subtle LED indicator (cool white or soft blue) will indicate power status and heating, with optimized brightness for visibility without distraction.; The indicator light will also flash red in conjunction with an audible tone for boil-dry protection alerts.; The indicator light's power consumption shall not exceed 0.1W.

The power base shall feature non-slip feet and be stable on a flat surface, withstanding a tilt angle of at least 15° without tipping.; The electrical connection between the kettle and base shall be robust, rated for a minimum of 3,000 connection cycles.; Weight distribution will be optimized for a low center of gravity to enhance overall stability.
